sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, 
you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be 
repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the 
best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are the exams corrected yet?
(man)No, but they'll be corrected by noon.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'll correct the exams this afternoon.
The exam will be at noon.
He will collect the exams at 12:00.
The tests will be graded by noon.

questions.passage:
(woman) Has Martha's visa arrived yet?
(man)I think it arrived last month.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha applied for a visa last month.
Martha's visa will last for only a month.
Martha arrived last month without her visa.
One month ago Martha got her visa.

questions.passage:
(man)What did the professor do in the first lass? I missed it because I was late.
(woman) She outlined the course requirements.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor described what the students should do.
There was a long line to register for the required class.
It is a requirement for each professor to teach at least one course.
The professor required the class to prepare an outline.

questions.passage:
(woman) How did Chuck look when you visited him in the hospital?
(man)He's looked better.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Chuck had improved.
This visit was better than the last.
Chuck looked at him in the hospital.
Chuck didn't seem to be doing very well.

questions.passage:
(man)How much was tuition increased for next month?
(woman) More than I can afford.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She thinks the tuition should be raised.
The semesters tuition is quite affordable.
She doesn't have enough money for her school fees.
She has more than enough for tuition.

questions.passage:
(woman) How were the grades on the history exam.
(man)No one got above a C.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks he got a good grade.
The history grades were all C or above.
No one got history grades.
There were no high scores.

questions.passage:
(woman) You know, this is the second time this week that you've been late to class!
(man)It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The parking lots were full before 10:00.
It was impossible to start class by 10:00.
He parked the car before class at 10:00.
The possibility of finding a place to park increased.

questions.passage:
(man)Can I help you find something?
(woman) Yes, thank you. I need to get a new rug.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's found a new ring.
She needs to help him find something.
She's shopping for a carpet.
She's thankful she has a rag.

questions.passage:
(woman) I'd like to open an account.
(man)Would you like a savings account or an interest-bearing checking account?
questions.question: 9. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSA TION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In a department store.
In a bank.
In an accounting firm.
In a checkout line.

questions.passage:
(woman) Why does Jane spend so much time in San Francisco?
(man)She has a cousin there, so she likes to visit, especially during the holidays.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Jane usually visits San Francisco for her vacations.
Jane's cousin often visits San Francisco.
Whenever there's a holiday, Jane's cousin goes to San Francisco.
Whenever there's a holiday, Jane leaves San Francisco.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you really hungry?
(man)I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks.
questions.question: 11.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'd really like to have something to eat.
Because he's weak, he can't eat.
It's been weeks since he's had anything to eat.
He hasn't felt like eating for weeks.

questions.passage:
(man)The traffic outside is really loud!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Traffic should not be allowed.
She thinks that the traffic should stay Outside.
She agrees that the traffic is noisy.
She'll stay outside with the man.

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you seen the headlines yet
today?
I haven't had a chance to read a word.
questions.question: 13.WHAT ARE THEY PROBABLY DIS CUSSING?

options.text: The headings for today's reading assignment.
The chance to make the headlines.
Her reading ability.
The daily newspaper.

questions.passage:
(man)I'm not ready yet, and it's going to take me a while longer.
(woman) You'd better hurry. Take five minutes too long, and you'll miss the bus.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The bus trip is only five minutes long.
The man missed the bus by five minutes.
The man should hurry to catch the bus.
The bus was five minutes late.

questions.passage:
(man)I think it's impossible for me to pass this class.
(woman) You should never say "impossible."
questions.question: 15. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: It's not possible to pass the class.
She'll definitely fail.
It's always possible.
She shouldn't say anything about the class.

questions.passage:
(Man)  Why were you thanking Tom?
(Man)  He lent me enough money to pay the rent.
questions.question: 16.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She gave Tom money to pay the rent.
She was given money for the rent.
Tom borrowed money for the rent.
She had some money to lend.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you enjoying the dessert?
(man)Never have I tasted such delicious  cake!
questions.question: 17.WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE CAKE?

options.text: The cake is extremely good.
He never tasted the cake.
He wished he hadn't tasted the cake.
The cake has never been very good.

questions.passage:
(man)Why are you so late getting here?
(woman) Oh, I ran into my cousin Carl, and we  stayed and talked for a while.
questions.question: 18.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: At the corner she ran into another car.
She ran to Carl because she cared.
She unexpectedly met one of her relatives.
Carl was running from place to place.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know where Debbie is?
(man)Her purse is still here, so she must still  be in the apartment.
questions.question: 19.WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT DEBBIE?

options.text: She shouldn't leave her purse here.
She's probably in the apartment.
Her purse must not be in the apartment.
She left without taking her purse.

questions.passage:
(man)Do you know when rent is due?
(woman) The landlord collects it on the first of the month, without fail.
questions.question: 20.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The landlord failed to collect rent on the first of last month.
The tenants absolutely must pay rent at the beginning of the month.
The landlord will not fail to collect your rent on the first of next month.
It is important to call the landlord about rent on the first of the month.

questions.passage:
(woman) My car is making some funny noises.
(man)Why not take it to a mechanic?
questions.question: 21.WHAT DOES THE MAN SUGGEST TO THE WOMAN?

options.text: Taking the car out for a test drive.
Listening to the noises.
Fixing the car herself.
Getting the car repaired.

questions.passage:
(man)Martha's holding down two jobs at the same time.
(woman) She'd better take it easy.
questions.question: 22.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha s jobs are easy.
It's easy to hold two jobs.
It's better for Martha to have two jobs.
Martha should slow down.

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you get to the airport in plenty of time?
(man)There was scarcely enough time to get
there.
questions.question: 23.WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: The plane took off just after he arrived.
He arrived just after the plane took off.
He wasn't in time to catch the plane.
He arrived too late to catch the plane.

questions.passage:
(woman) You should put some money in the
(man)parking meter.
Parking fees aren't necessary on the
weekend, are they?
questions.question: 24.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He agrees with the woman's suggestion.
Parking is not free on the weekend.
It is not necessary for them to park.
He thinks they don't have to pay.

questions.passage:
(MAN) How is your boss feeling about his retirement?
(WOMEN) Oh, he isn't too unhappy to be retiing.
questions.question: 25. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY ABOUT HER BOSS?

options.text: He is eager to leave his job.
He is unhappy at the thought of retiring.
He couldn't be unhappier about retiring.
He is retiring too soon.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Oh, I see you have a new car.
(WOMEN) Oh, he isn't too unhappy to be retiing.
questions.question: 26. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He got the car he really wanted.
He didn't get a new car.
The car that he got was not his first choice.
He didn't really want a new car.

questions.passage:
(WOMEN)Did you hear the president's announcement this morning?
(MAN) Yes, the president appointed Mr. Drew head of the newly formed commission.
questions.question: 27. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Mr. Drew pointedly asked the president about the committee.
The president pointed to Mr. Drew's head.
Mr. Drew became head of the new commission.
Mr. Drew was committed to the president's appointments.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Were you upset by what Richard said to vou?
(WOMAN) I couldn't have been more infuriated!
questions.question: 28. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She felt inferior.
She wasn't furious.
She felt there should have been more fairness.
She was extremely angry.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Let me just get these last plates put away. Then, I'll be ready to go.
(WOMAN) So you did do the dishes.
questions.question: 29. WHAT HAD THE WOMAN ASSUMED?

options.text: The man would do the dishes.
The plates did not need to be washed.
The man would not be ready to go.
The dishes would not be done.

questions.passage:
(WOMAN) Why did you get that kind of fruit?
(MAN) I wouldn't have bought these cherries had I known that grapes were so cheap.
questions.question: 30. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He knew that grapes were cheaper than cherries.
He didn't know that grapes were cheaper than cherries.
He bought grapes because they were cheaper than cherries.
He didn't buy either grapes or cherries because of the price.

parts.title: Part B
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear longer conversations. After each conversation, you will hear several questions. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. 

After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

questions.question: 31. WHAT IS THE WOMAN PLANNING TO DO SATURDAY?

options.text: Attend a football game alone.
Go to a sporting event.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: Eat in the cafeteria and study.
See a play.

questions.question: 32. WHY DOES THE MAN WANT TO GO TO THE FOOTBALL GAME?

options.text: It's the final game of the season.
It's better than the drama department's play.
It's a very important game.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: It's close to the cafeteria.

questions.question: 33. WHAT IS AT THE SAME TIME AS THE MUSIC DEPARTMENT'S CONCERT?

options.text: A play.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: A game.
A study group meeting.
Dinner in the cafeteria.

questions.question: 34. WHEN DOES THE MAN PLAN TO STUDY?

options.text: Saturday night.
After dinner in the cafeteria.
Sunday afternoon.
Maybe next weekend.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

questions.question: 35. WHAT ARE THE MAN AND WOMAN DISCUSSING?

options.text: Trash orbiting Earth.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: A trip by an astronaut to the Moon.
The overabundance of garbage on Earth.
Becoming space scientists.

questions.question: 36. WHERE DID THE WOMAN LEARN ABOUT THIS PROBLEM?

options.text: From a lecture.
In a magazine article.
In a book.
On a television program.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

questions.question: 37. APPROXIMATELY HOW MUCH METAL IS IN ORBIT IN SPACE?

options.text: 17,000 pounds.
3,000 tons.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: 3,000 pounds.
300 tons.

questions.question: 38. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN HOPE WILL HAPPEN?

options.text: She will be able to travel in space.
The problem will take care of itself.
Scientists will find solutions to theproblem.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: The junk will fall to Earth.

groups.passage:
Questions 31-34

(narrator)    Listen to a conversation between two friends who are making plans.
(woman)       Do you have any plans this weekend? There's so much to choose from on campus that I'm not sure what I'm going to do.
(man)         The football game's on Saturday night, and I'm going with a group of friends. Do you want to go with us?
(woman)       Of course I'd like to go to the football game: it's the biggest game of the sea son. And it sounds like fun to go with a large group of people.
(man)         Good. We'll be meeting at the cafete ria for dinner at six o'clock on Satur day night, and then we'll go on to the game together.
(woman)       That takes care of my plans for Satur day night. But now I need to make a decision about Sunday afternoon. The music department is sponsoring a concert then, and I'd really like to hear that concert. But there's also a play being presented by the drama department that I really wanted to see. It's too bad those two events are at the same time.
(man)         You know, if you go to the game on Saturday night and a concert or play on Sunday, that doesn't leave much time for studying.
(woman)       Oh, well. Maybe I can do that the weekend after this one.
groups.label: Q31-34

groups.passage:
Questions 35-38

(narrator)    I saw a television program about it last night, and according to the program, there's about 3,000 tons of metal out there in space, traveling at speeds around 17,000 miles per hour.
(man)         Where did all this garbage come from?
(woman)       Well, it comes from all those space missions that have gone up since 1957. Every time a rocket ship goes up into space, it leaves a lot behind, and this stuff goes into orbit around the Earth: booster rockets, solar panels, remnants of satellites, and even nuclear reactors.
(man)         Isn't it dangerous to have all this stuff out there?
(woman)       Some space scientists are worried about possible collisions between this orbiting junk and spaceships, particu larly manned spacecrafts; however, so far there haven't been any such acci dents.
(man)         Well, I hope that they're going to do something about this, both for the sake of safety and for the sake of the environment.
(woman)       Me, too. I know that right now the problem is being studied by numer ous scientists; hopefully, they'll be able to find solutions before the prob lem gets too much worse.
groups.label: Q35-38

parts.title: Part C
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear several talks. After each talk, you will hear some 
questions. The talks and questions will not be repeated. 
After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best 
answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Here is an example. 
On the recording, you hear: 
(narrator) Listen to an instructor talk to his class about painting. 
(man) Artist Grant Wood was a guiding force in the school'of painting known as American 
regionalist, a style reflecting the distinctive characteristics of art from rural areas of 
the United States. Wood began drawing animals on the family farm at the age of three, 
and when he was thirty-eight one of his paintings received a remarkable amount of 
. public notice and acclaim. This painting, called "American Gothic," is a starkly 
simple depiction of a serious couple staring directly out at the viewer. 

Now listen to a sample question.
(narrator) What style of painting is known as American regionalist?

In your test book, you read:
(A) Art from America s inner cities
(B) Art from the central region of the United States 
(C) Art from various urban areas in the United States 
(D) Art from rural sections of America 

The best answer to the question "What style of painting is known as American regionalist?" is (D), "Art from rural sections of America." Therefore, the correct choice is (D). 

Now listen to another sample question.
(narrator) What is the name of Wood's most successful painting? 
In your test book, you read:
(A) "American Regionalist"
(B) "The Family Farm in Iowa"
(C) "American Gothic" 
(D) "A Serious Couple" 

The best answer to the question, "What is the name of Wood's most successful painting?" is (C), "American Gothic." Therefore, the correct choice is (C). 

Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

questions.question: 39.  WHEN DOES THIS TALK PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: On the first day of class
In the middle of the semester

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: At the end of class
In the final week of the semester
Later today
By Friday of this week
In two weeks

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: In three weeks

options.text: Journal and magazine articles
Books from outside the library

options.text: Two
Three
Five

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: In winter
In spring

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: In summer

options.text: Seasonable, with warm summers and cold winters
Fairly constant and moderate

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: Very humid
Extremely hot year-round

questions.question: 45.  WHAT IS TRUE ABOUT THE TRADE WINDS?

options.text: They come from the Southwest.
They come most days of the year.

reason.en: Correct answer based on the conversation.

options.text: They are the hardest during the night.
They increase the humidity.

questions.question:
WHAT WILL THE PEOPLE LISTENING TO THE 
TALK PROBABLY BE DOING SOON?

options.text: Preparing for a trip
Writing a report about the weather
Beginning a study of the weather
Buying warm clothes for a trip

questions.question: 47.  THIS LECTURE WOULD PROBABLY BE A PART OF WHICH COURSE?

options.text: Modern American Authors
United States History
American Democracy

questions.question: 48.  WHAT IS THE MOST COMMON THEME IN LEAVES OF GRASS?

options.text: The death of Abraham Lincoln
The beauty of American democracy
The raising of plants
The maturity of poetry

questions.question: 49.  WHAT BEST DESCRIBES LEAVES OF GRASS}

options.text: It's a poem about the author.
It's a poem about Abraham Lincoln.
It's a collection of twelve poems that remained unchanged.
It's a volume of poetry that grew with its author.

questions.question: 50.  WHICH IS THE LONGEST AND BEST-KNOWN POEM BYWHITMAN?

options.text: "Leaves of Grass"
"Song of Myself"
"When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed"

groups.passage:
Questions 39-42

(narrator)    Listen to a sociology professor talk to her class.
(woman)       Before I start today's sociology lec ture, I'd like to talk with you about the papers that you should be working on. As you know, the topic for the paper is the relationship between gun control and violence. The paper itself is due in two weeks, but I would like to see your oudines by Friday of this week so that I can be sure that you are on the right track with the assign ment.
              You need to do some research for this paper, so you should be spending some time in the library. I would like you to have at least three books and at least three recent journal articles as sources.
              The paper should be five pages long; in addition to the five pages of com position, you should have a tide page and a one-page reference list of the sources that you used in preparing the paper.
groups.label: Q39-42

groups.passage:
Questions 43-46

(narrator)    Listen to a talk about Hawaii.
(woman)	For those of you taking part in the trip to Hawaii next week, I'd like to give you a litde information about the weather that you can expect there. You can expect the average daily tem perature there to be about 80 degrees Fahrenheit or 26 degrees Celsius. This is the average daily temperature in the springtime, when we will be there; it is interesting to note that it only gets a few degrees warmer in the summer and a few degrees cooler in the win ter. One important factor that keeps the temperature so constant and moder ate in Hawaii is the trade winds. These are winds that blow in on the north east, or windward, side of the islands on an almost daily basis; the trade winds blow through the islands an average of slighdy more than 300 days per year, and they are the strongest during the heat of the afternoon and turn into a cooling breeze in the evening. The trade winds also keep the humidity down, which makes the weather even more pleasant. I hope this information will help you to understand the weather conditions that you're going to encounter next week on your trip; it should also help you decide what types of clothes you should be packing for your trip.
groups.label: Q43-45

sections.instruction: In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.
